chief_complaint: A 40-year-old female presented with nasal crusting, epistaxis, fever, weight loss, haemoptysis, bilateral pulmonary nodules, and a pancreatic tail mass.
history: Granulomatosis with polyangiitis (GPA), a subtype of antineutrophil cytoplasmic antibody (ANCA)-associated vasculitis (AAV), is a systemic inflammatory disease. The patient developed haematuria and proteinuria, and proteinase 3-ANCA testing and renal biopsy confirmed AAV. The patient had been treated with antitubercular therapy initially.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Pancreatic biopsy revealed caseating granulomatous inflammation. Renal biopsy confirmed AAV. Proteinase 3-ANCA testing was positive.
